error.signature: RuntimeError: CUDA error: peer access is not supported between these two devices

verdict.summary: GPUs cannot directly access each other's memory. No NVLink between the devices or PCIe topology prevents P2P.

dead_ends.action: Force enable peer access with environment variables
dead_ends.why_fails: P2P is a hardware capability; if the bus topology doesn't support it, no software config can enable it

dead_ends.action: Move all tensors to a single GPU to avoid P2P
dead_ends.why_fails: Defeats the purpose of multi-GPU; model may not fit on one GPU

workarounds.action: Disable P2P and let NCCL fall back to shared host memory
workarounds.how: export NCCL_P2P_DISABLE=1  # uses host-staged copies instead

workarounds.action: Check GPU topology to verify which GPUs have P2P support
workarounds.how: nvidia-smi topo -m  # NV# = NVLink, PHB = no direct P2P

workarounds.action: Place communicating tensors on GPUs with direct NVLink connections

preceded_by.condition: Failed P2P access causes NCCL to timeout on collectives
